nodes.label: SendMessage-First Agent Comms Pattern
nodes.rationale: Named agents coordinate directly via SendMessage rather than polling or shared state, to avoid coordination overhead in pipeline/fan-out/supervisor patterns.

nodes.label: 3-Tier Model Routing (Agent Booster / Haiku / Sonnet-Opus)
nodes.rationale: Routes simple transforms to WASM/Haiku tiers and complex architecture/security reasoning to Sonnet/Opus to balance cost and capability.

nodes.label: SendMessage-First Agent Comms Pattern (Claude)
nodes.rationale: Named agents coordinate directly via SendMessage rather than polling or shared state.

nodes.label: Contributing to Autonomous RAG Optimizer Guide
nodes.norm_label: contributing to autonomous rag optimizer guide

nodes.label: Protocol-based Dependency Injection
nodes.rationale: All external dependencies (LLM clients, databases, cost trackers) are abstracted behind Python Protocol interfaces in src/core/ so the Provider DI container can swap implementations for testing/multi-provider support.

nodes.label: ThreadPool Isolation for RAGAS
nodes.rationale: Since RAGAS runs blocking sync evaluation, it is offloaded to a ThreadPoolExecutor with nest_asyncio to avoid deadlocking the main asyncio loop.

nodes.label: Strict Budget Guarding Feature
nodes.rationale: Intercepts and monitors LLM (OpenRouter) API costs to prevent runaway billing during long-running optimization sessions.

nodes.label: Budget Guard Security Scrutiny Requirement
nodes.rationale: budget_guard_node enforces hard cost ceilings; a bug here can cause runaway billing, so changes to budget logic in src/orchestrator/ should be reviewed with extra scrutiny.

nodes.label: LLM Prompt Injection Concern
nodes.rationale: The Scientist node feeds historical experiment data into LLM prompts; corpus data or config values must not be able to inject malicious instructions into prompt templates.

nodes.label: OpenAI Pricing Table (USD per million tokens)
nodes.norm_label: openai pricing table (usd per million tokens)

nodes.label: Manual Pricing Update Rationale
nodes.rationale: OpenAI's API does not expose pricing programmatically, so this file must be updated by hand when OpenAI changes prices; a model missing from the list logs a warning and is costed at $0 rather than failing the run.

nodes.label: ThreadPool Solution for RAGAS/asyncio Deadlock
nodes.rationale: To prevent ragas from blocking the main asyncio event loop, the Evaluator node offloads evaluation calls to a ThreadPool Executor and applies nest_asyncio in the background thread to avoid RuntimeError: This event loop is already running.

nodes.label: Cost Tracker Constructor Injection Decision
nodes.rationale: A Decorator-pattern wrapper around ILLMClient.call() was considered and rejected because call()'s return type carries no token-usage data and the codebase makes concurrent LLM calls, so smuggling cost via instance state would race; constructor injection of cost_tracker was the correct fix instead.

nodes.label: Reranker Doesn't Fit Provider-Lifetime Singleton
nodes.rationale: OpenRouterRerank is constructed fresh per retrieval call with top_n=config.reranker_top_n baked in and consumed as a LlamaIndex postprocess_nodes()-shaped object via RerankingRetriever, not through IReranker.rerank()'s generic interface shape; top_n is a per-RAGConfig value, so a Provider-lifetime singleton doesn't fit.

nodes.label: Embedding Model Is a Per-Experiment RAGConfig Value
nodes.rationale: config.embedding_model is searched over by the scientist, not a fixed setting; Provider is constructed once before any RAGConfig exists, so baking a specific embedding model into Provider at construction time would be fragile given VALID_EMBEDDING_MODELS could grow beyond one entry.
nodes.norm_label: embedding model is a per-experiment ragconfig value

nodes.label: Factory-Style Wiring Design Option
nodes.rationale: Both embeddings and reranking need a factory-style API (config values in, provider-appropriate concrete instance out) rather than a clean Provider singleton attach, because the per-experiment config values determine which provider/model to use.

nodes.label: Autonomous RAG Optimizer Step-by-Step Overnight Run Guide
nodes.norm_label: autonomous rag optimizer step-by-step overnight run guide

nodes.label: State Isolation Architecture Rationale
nodes.rationale: Each node is a pure function taking a state dict and outputting modifications, making it clean to unit test individual nodes in isolation.

nodes.label: Crash Resilience via SQLite Checkpointer
nodes.rationale: The SQLite checkpointer saves state at every step so a crashed or interrupted run can resume without losing hours of evaluations.
